Title: Pyke
Age: Current31 – 51 years old Timeline Born946 AN – 966 AN Edit

children.title: Early life
children.textContent:
Before death claimed him, Pyke was just another dockhand in Bilgewater's infamous slaughter docks... the Red Dock, where coin was scarce and danger plentiful. He quickly adapted to the brutal rhythm of the trade, hauling sea beasts for scraps. But Pyke hungered for more than survival. Each time a crew returned with monstrous catches, his ambition swelled. Eventually, his relentless drive earned him a spot aboard a hunting vessel.

Pyke excelled in the deadly art of Buhru-style harpoon hunting, rising to fame as one of the finest harpooners in Bilgewater. His precision and ruthlessness were unmatched.

Then came the Jaull Fish.

A legendary leviathan whose magical oils were worth a fortune, the Jaull Fish surfaced during one of Pyke's voyages. Without hesitation, Pyke dove into its gaping maw, determined to slay the beast. But the creature let out a deafening screech, summoning a swarm of its kind that battered the ship. In a moment of panic, the captain severed Pyke's lifeline, sacrificing him to save the crew.

As Pyke sank into darkness, he glimpsed the terrified faces of his crewmates. Faces that would haunt him forever.

But death didn't claim Pyke.

Inside the belly of the beast, something ancient and arcane awakened. Lights watched him. Whispers tore through his mind. Visions of betrayal consumed him—visions of his comrades profiting from his demise. The Jaull Fish's oils seeped into his flesh, warping him, granting him unnatural powers and a singular purpose: punishment.

Pyke clawed his way out of the abyss and returned to Bilgewater. Not as a man, but as a specter of vengeance.

children.title: Contemporary history
children.textContent:
Soon after Pyke's return, whispers of death began to echo through Bilgewater's alleys. At first, the killings were dismissed—just another night in a city soaked in blood. But as the bodies piled up, a pattern emerged.

Captains were found mutilated in their beds, their throats slit, their eyes wide with terror. Always at night. Always silent.

Then it wasn't just captains.

Anyone tied to the corrupt dealings of the Red Dock: smugglers, slavers, crooked merchants—all began to vanish. The city trembled. A bounty was placed on the killer's head, but no one dared speak his name aloud.

...

They gave him a name that would haunt everybody: the Red Dock Ripper.

children.title: Ruined King
children.textContent:
Due to the fear he instilled in Bilgewater, Illaoi, the high priestess of the Buhru, decided to put a stop to the Red Dock Ripper, leaving the Serpent Isles and venturing into Bilgewater to try to track him down. When she was about to kill a retired captain, Pyke encountered Illaoi and her followers. At first he did not see interest in killing her, but when he saw how insistent she was on stopping him, he prepared to fight her.

The battle was fierce, but Illaoi finally managed to overpower Pyke. Seeing that he was an undead, she felt disgusted since the undead were a blasphemy to her religion. So she subjected him to the test of Nagakabouros knowing that due to his undead nature he would not survive and would be crushed and destroyed by the power of the Goddess.

However, Pyke managed to survive, because to the confusion of Illaoi, the Goddess considered him worthy. Pyke was left stunned and with his mind spinning wondering what she had done to him, but he still had enough strength to escape and fled from a disconcerted Illaoi.

mainContent.title: Appearance
mainContent.textContent: Pyke is a corpse-like entity that has been in the water for quite some time. He is thin, skeletal, has slightly pale dark skin, a shaved head, and bright blue-green lights that shine in his sockets as eyes.

mainContent.title: Personality
mainContent.textContent:
In life, Pyke was a very loyal and hard-working man who was willing to do anything to be recognized. After dying and resurrecting he became a living killing machine, he was ruthless, sadistic and very vengeful, he was determined to take revenge on those responsible for his death.

After Illaoi tested him and proved him worthy, Pyke regained some of his former self, always sharpening his knife to avoid falling into a fit of rage.

children.title: Captains
children.textContent: Miss Fortune and Gangplank are both on Pyke's list, both being captains. He would later hunt Miss Fortune during Rise of the Sentinels, when the Sentinels told her she was on his list.

children.title: Crew of the Charming Lady
children.textContent: During the events of Ruined King, Ahri, Braum, Illaoi, Miss Fortune, Pyke and Yasuo would set sail to the Shadow Isles to fight Viego in order to protect Bilgewater. Although Pyke is an undead, Illaoi sees Pyke as an eddy of a much deeper tide and therefore is in motion. She subjects Pyke to Nagakabouros' test and her influence calms him from his murderous states. Later, Pyke saved the Sentinels of Light from Miss Fortune's attack after she made a deal with Viego in exchange for power. Pyke then joined the team despite most of the members not approving it.

children.title: Olaf
children.textContent: Olaf wanted to fight Pyke, thinking he could grant him a warrior's death, and said he cut his line.

mainContent.textContent:
